data.title: Airbus targets 820 aircraft deliveries and €7B EBIT adjusted for 2025 amid supply chain challenges
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Airbus SE (OTCPK:EADSF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Guillaume Faury highlighted a strong order intake across all businesses, with 878 gross orders in 2024, including 211 in Q4. The A321XLR entered into service, unlocking new opportunities for customers. He emphasized that Airbus delivered 766 commercial aircraft in 2024, including 269 in Q4, reflecting a 4% year-on-year increase.
  • Faury noted a focus on transforming the Defence and Space division, including exploratory discussions with Thales and Leonardo to evaluate strategic options for consolidating the European space sector.
  • CFO Thomas Toepfer reported full-year revenues of €69.2 billion, a 6% year-on-year increase, driven by higher deliveries and growth in the Air Power business. EBIT adjusted for 2024 amounted to €5.4 billion, impacted by €1.3 billion in charges related to space programs.
  • Toepfer announced a dividend proposal of €2 per share and a special dividend of €1 per share, reflecting confidence in future financial performance.

Outlook

  • Airbus targets around 820 commercial aircraft deliveries, EBIT adjusted of approximately €7 billion, and free cash flow before customer financing of €4.5 billion for 2025.
  • The company plans to ramp up production rates for the A320 family to 75 per month by 2027, while the A350 freighter variant’s entry-into-service is now expected in the second half of 2027.
  • Management acknowledged ongoing supply chain challenges, particularly with Spirit AeroSystems and engine suppliers, which are expected to impact Q1 2025 deliveries but are anticipated to normalize in the second half of the year.

Financial Results

  • Full-year 2024 revenues reached €69.2 billion, supported by increased aircraft and helicopter deliveries and higher volumes in the Air Power business.
  • EBIT adjusted for 2024 stood at €5.4 billion, reflecting commercial aircraft deliveries and strong helicopter performance, offset by charges in the space division and less favorable hedge rates.
  • Free cash flow before customer financing was €4.5 billion, supported by robust commercial momentum, although negatively impacted by the A400M program and inventory buildup for ramp-ups.
  • The net income for 2024 was €4.2 billion, with earnings per share reported at €5.36. Adjusted EPS stood at €5.05 based on an average of 790 million shares.

Q&A

  • Benjamin Heelan, Bank of America: Asked about Spirit AeroSystems' operational challenges. CFO Thomas Toepfer explained that a strike at Boeing and the need for additional investments to support ramp-ups for the A350 and A220 were key issues. He stated the integration of Spirit’s work packages is expected to close by mid-year.
  • David Perry, JPMorgan: Inquired about delivery guidance for 2025. CEO Guillaume Faury declined to specify model-level projections but highlighted bottlenecks with Spirit AeroSystems and engine suppliers affecting the A350 and A220 ramp-ups.
  • Doug Harned, Bernstein: Queried the A350 ramp-up impact from Spirit versus interior suppliers. Faury clarified that Spirit’s issues directly hinder ramp-up, while interior delays primarily affect delivery timing.
  • Ian Douglas-Pennant, UBS: Asked about the long-term cash flow outlook for Spirit AeroSystems. Toepfer stated that after investments and process improvements, Spirit’s work packages are expected to achieve cost parity with current external sourcing beyond 2028.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed skepticism regarding supply chain challenges, particularly Spirit AeroSystems, and sought clarity on operational and financial impacts. Questions reflected concerns about ramp-up feasibility for key programs like the A350 and A220.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone. CEO Guillaume Faury expressed confidence in meeting 2025 delivery targets but acknowledged ongoing bottlenecks. CFO Thomas Toepfer provided detailed explanations on financial impacts, including Spirit-related costs and dividend decisions.
  • Compared to prepared remarks, management was more defensive in addressing operational risks during the Q&A, particularly regarding supply chain constraints and Spirit AeroSystems’ integration.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • A comparison to the previous quarter is unavailable due to the lack of the prior quarter’s transcript.
  • Management emphasized consistent strategic priorities, including ramping up aircraft production, addressing supply chain challenges, and transforming the Defence and Space division.
  • Analysts’ tone remained focused on supply chain risks, reflecting ongoing concerns about operational execution.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted supply chain disruptions, particularly with Spirit AeroSystems and engine suppliers, as risks to achieving ramp-up targets for the A350 and A220.
  • Airbus faces potential challenges from tariffs, which could impact supply chain stability and financial performance.
  • Delays in the entry-into-service for the A350 freighter and ongoing issues with engine availability were noted as additional risks.

Final Takeaway

Airbus SE reported solid 2024 financial results, supported by strong commercial aircraft and helicopter deliveries. Looking ahead, the company targets 820 aircraft deliveries and €7 billion EBIT adjusted for 2025, despite ongoing supply chain challenges. Management remains focused on ramping up production and integrating Spirit AeroSystems' work packages to secure operational stability. Analysts raised concerns about supply chain risks, but management expressed confidence in navigating these challenges to achieve long-term growth.

data.title: Live Nation Entertainment reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Live Nation Entertainment press release (NYSE:LYV): Q4 Revenue of $5.68B (-2.7% Y/Y) beats by $10M.
  • Operating income of $825 million
  • Adjusted operating income of $2.15 billion
  • Adjusted operating income for concerts at a record high, up 65% to $530 million with margins of 2.8%
  • Concert attendance up 4%, with 151 million fans attending over 50k Live Nation events, up 9%
  • Sponsorship adjusted operating income of $764 million, up 13%

data.title: Texas Roadhouse tops estimates as comparable sales and margins shine
data.description:
Texas Roadhouse (NASDAQ:TXRH) slid past profit estimates with its Q4 earnings report on revenue growth of 23.3%.

Comparable restaurant sales increased 7.7% at company restaurants and increased 6.3% at domestic franchise restaurants. Average weekly sales at company restaurants were $153,867 of which $20,067 were to-go sales vs. average weekly sales of $141,653 of which $17,793 were to-go sales in the prior year.

Restaurant margin as a percentage of sales increased to 17.0% from 15.3% in the prior year, driven primarily by higher sales. The benefit of a higher average guest check, the benefit of the additional week, and improved labor productivity more than offset wage and other labor inflation of 5.0% and commodity inflation of 0.3%.

Nine company restaurants and five franchise restaurants were opened during the quarter.

"We ended 2024 on an incredible note, which was highlighted by fourth quarter and full-year traffic growth at all three of our brands." highlighted CEO Jerry Morgan.

Morgan said the company is off to a strong start in 2025 with the completion of the previously announced acquisition of 13 franchise restaurants. "In addition, due to the continued growth across our portfolio, our 800th restaurant is under construction and will open later this year," he added. Texas Roadhouse (NASDAQ:TXRH) plans to leverage the strength of its balance sheet to continue to fund development growth while also returning capital to our shareholders.

The new year is off to a solid start for Texas Roadhouse (TXRH). Comparable restaurant sales at company restaurants for the first seven weeks of FQ1 increased 2.9% compared to 2024. In addition, the company plans to implement a menu price increase of approximately 1.4% in early April.

Shares of Texas Roadhouse (TXRH) were down 1.0% in postmarket action.

data.title: MercadoLibre GAAP EPS of $12.61 beats by $5.05, revenue of $6.06B beats by $120M
data.description:
• MercadoLibre press release (NASDAQ:MELI): Q4 GAAP EPS of $12.61 beats by $5.05.
  • Revenue of $6.06B (+37.4% Y/Y) beats by $120M.
  • $58.9 billion Total Payment Volume, up 33% YoY and 49% FX-neutral $14.5 billion
  • Gross Merchandise Volume, up 8% YoY and 56% FX-neutral.
  • We ended 2024 with fantastic momentum and we are optimistic as we look ahead to 2025 and beyond. Investments that we have been making for years in our value proposition are bearing fruit and extending our competitive advantages. We are seeing tangible evidence of the power of the ecosystem as each part of it strengthens engagement in the others. This puts us in a unique position to explore the significant growth opportunities offered by the shift of offline retail online, by providing better financial services to millions of people and by the growing importance of first party data in advertising. We are as confident as ever that the best is yet to come.
  • Shares +13.24%.

data.title: Innergex projects 13% EBITDA growth to $875M in 2025 driven by new project contributions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Innergex Renewable Energy, Inc. (OTCPK:INGXF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Michel Letellier, President and CEO, highlighted that the company achieved $761 million of EBITDA for 2024, surpassing its target. Free cash flow per share exceeded guidance, reaching $1.06. He attributed these results to strong operational efficiency with a 96% asset availability.
  • Letellier noted the successful commissioning of the Boswell Springs wind project, a 330 MW wind farm in Wyoming operating under a 30-year PPA. Development on the Innavik hydro facility and progress on the Hale Kuawehi solar and battery project in Hawaii were also emphasized.
  • Three projects in British Columbia were secured, with indexed PPAs tied to the CPI, marking the company’s first involvement in the region in 14 years. Additionally, 1.3 GW of prospective projects were added to the portfolio, with management expressing confidence in growth opportunities in Canada.
  • CFO Jean Trudel stated, "We reported adjusted EBITDA proportionate of $210 million in Q4 2024, and $761 million for the full year, approximately 13% and 3% higher year over year respectively." He emphasized cost discipline, commissioning of new assets, and one-time recoverable production tax credits as key contributors.

Outlook

  • Innergex provided 2025 guidance, projecting adjusted EBITDA proportionate between $825 million and $875 million, representing a $100 million increase compared to 2024 guidance.
  • Free cash flow per share is expected to range between $0.75 and $0.95, with growth driven by the contributions of Boswell Springs and new battery energy storage projects in Chile.
  • The company plans to bid over 500 MW of projects in 2025, targeting wins of approximately 400 MW, with a focus on Canada due to strong market opportunities.
  • Management cited production expectations in line with long-term averages and asset availability around 95% as key assumptions for 2025.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EBITDA proportionate for Q4 2024 reached $210 million, benefiting from above-average hydro generation in British Columbia and the commissioning of the Boswell Springs wind project.
  • Free cash flow was $49.2 million for the quarter, amounting to $0.24 per share. For the full year, free cash flow totaled $214 million or $1.06 per share.
  • Total debt stood at $6.6 billion, with liquidity improving to over $700 million due to $450 million in completed financing and a $250 million sell-down of Texas assets.
  • The company highlighted a self-amortizing debt structure and a focus on maintaining its investment-grade profile.

Q&A

  • Nick Boychuk, Cormark: "How are you thinking about allocating capital between Canada and other markets?" Michel Letellier: "We have great opportunities in Canada, but our teams in France and Chile also demonstrate strong independence and ability to create value."
  • Nelson Ng, RBC Capital Markets: "What’s the total capex for the five projects under construction?" Jean Trudel: "It varies but ranges from $3 million to $3.6 million per megawatt for wind projects."
  • Rupert Merer, National Bank: "Can you discuss the implications of current geopolitical risks on your U.S. projects like Palomino?" Michel Letellier: "We’ve secured panels and Safe Harbor provisions for tax credits, ensuring flexibility to adapt to policy changes."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about geopolitical risks and resource allocation, particularly between Canadian and international markets. Their tone was cautiously optimistic but focused on risks.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, emphasizing operational achievements and flexibility to manage potential challenges. During the Q&A, they were slightly defensive, particularly regarding U.S. market uncertainties, using phrases like "we have the flexibility" and "we are positioned."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone reflected increased confidence, supported by strong financial results and strategic wins.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language shifted positively, with 2025 EBITDA guidance reflecting higher growth expectations compared to earlier projections.
  • Analysts’ focus evolved towards geopolitical risks and the allocation of capital among markets, compared to prior concerns around project execution.
  • Management’s confidence increased, particularly regarding secured projects in Canada and the successful commissioning of Boswell Springs.
  • Financial results improved quarter-over-quarter, with higher adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow figures, aided by one-time recoveries and new project contributions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Analysts questioned the impact of U.S. policy changes on tax credits and tariffs for the Palomino project. Management stated they have secured Safe Harbor provisions and remain flexible to adjust pricing as needed.
  • Concerns over inflationary pressures and currency exchange risks were addressed by management, who cited hedging strategies and contingencies in project bids.
  • The company faces competition in Chile’s battery storage market, with increasing applications potentially pressuring arbitrage opportunities. Management plans to embed battery storage into long-term PPA strategies to mitigate risks.

Final Takeaway

Innergex Renewable Energy delivered strong Q4 2024 results, highlighted by above-guidance free cash flow and successful project completions like Boswell Springs. The company’s 2025 guidance reflects ambitious growth, with a projected 13% increase in adjusted EBITDA and expanded opportunities in Canada and Chile. While geopolitical risks and competitive pressures remain, Innergex’s operational efficiency, disciplined capital allocation, and focus on long-term PPAs position it well for future growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ryan Cohen raises Alibaba stake to $1B - WSJ
data.description:
Ryan Cohen, the investor known for GameStop (GME), has raised his stake in Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) to about $1 billion.

Cohen, who is also the founder of pet retailer Chewy (CHWY), owns about 7 million Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) shares, according to a WSJ report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. Cohen is the chairman and CEO of GamesStop.

The WSJ reported in January 2023 activist investor Cohen had amassed a stake in Alibaba (BABA) worth hundreds of millions of dollars.

Cohen has gained fame in recent years, especially for his stake in GameStop, specifically among meme-stock traders.

Shares of Alibaba (BABA) rose 8.7% on Thursday as Alibaba (BABA) reported Q3 results that beat estimates.

data.title: Cheniere targets $6.5B-$7B EBITDA in 2025 with expanded LNG capacity
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cheniere Energy, Inc. (NYSE:LNG) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Jack Fusco highlighted Cheniere's record-breaking performance in 2024, producing approximately 45 million tons of LNG, accounting for over 10% of global supply. He emphasized the company's financial discipline, achieving $6.155 billion in consolidated adjusted EBITDA and $3.73 billion in distributable cash flow for the year.
  • Fusco announced plans to advance Corpus Christi Stage 3, with Train 1 commissioning progressing on schedule. The first three trains are expected to ramp up production by the end of 2025, contributing to Cheniere's growth trajectory.
  • The company introduced its 2025 financial guidance, projecting $6.5 billion to $7 billion in consolidated adjusted EBITDA and $4.1 billion to $4.6 billion in distributable cash flow, signaling a rebound from 2024 as a trough year.
  • CFO Zach Davis noted that Cheniere repurchased nearly 14 million shares in 2024 for approximately $2.25 billion, increased its dividend by 15% to $2 per share annualized, and deployed over $1.5 billion towards the Stage 3 project.
  • The company announced new $4 billion share repurchase authorization and highlighted its focus on maintaining robust financial flexibility while continuing infrastructure expansion.

Outlook

  • Cheniere reaffirmed its 2025 LNG production forecast of 47 million to 48 million tons, driven by the operation of its nine trains and initial production from Corpus Christi Stage 3 trains.
  • The first three trains of Corpus Christi Stage 3 are expected to reach substantial completion throughout 2025. Management sees these developments boosting financial performance, with EBITDA guidance for 2025 reaching the $6.5 billion to $7 billion range.
  • The company anticipates maintaining high contracting levels, with over 90% of its platform contracted through the mid-2030s, ensuring steady cash flows.
  • Management expressed optimism regarding a constructive regulatory environment, enabling further long-term permitting for LNG projects in the U.S.

Financial Results

  • Cheniere reported $1.6 billion in consolidated adjusted EBITDA and $1 billion in net income for Q4 2024, contributing to its full-year results of $6.155 billion in EBITDA and $3.3 billion in net income.
  • Distributable cash flow for the quarter totaled $1.1 billion, bringing the annual figure to $3.73 billion, surpassing the high end of the original guidance range by $300 million.
  • The company highlighted its record LNG production, achieved while completing maintenance at Sabine Pass and Corpus Christi facilities.
  • Execution on the 2020 Vision capital allocation plan resulted in $5.4 billion deployed in 2024 across shareholder returns, growth initiatives, and debt reduction.

Q&A

  • Theresa Chen, Barclays: Questioned geopolitical impacts on U.S. LNG and long-term contracts with European customers. CEO Fusco emphasized energy security and diversity as priorities, stating that the conflict has underscored the importance of reliable LNG supply, with Anatol Feygin adding that Cheniere's flexible product remains a preferred solution for Europe.
  • Justin Jenkins, Raymond James: Asked about regulatory and permitting developments under the new administration. Fusco described the administration's actions as "refreshing" and supportive of Cheniere's accelerated project timelines.
  • Jeremy Tonet, JPMorgan: Queried financial sensitivity to Corpus Christi Stage 3 commissioning volumes. Davis outlined the production forecast of 47 million to 48 million tons, with Train 1 substantial completion expected by Q1 2025 and subsequent trains contributing significantly in the latter half of the year.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts maintained a neutral to positive tone, focusing on Cheniere's operational execution and strategic positioning amid geopolitical volatility. Questions highlighted confidence in the company's ability to navigate uncertain markets while capitalizing on growth opportunities.
  • Management displayed confidence during prepared remarks, emphasizing operational milestones and financial achievements. The tone during Q&A sessions remained assured, with detailed responses addressing concerns and reinforcing Cheniere's competitive advantages.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone reflected heightened optimism due to tangible progress in Corpus Christi Stage 3 and favorable market dynamics.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 2024 reflected higher adjusted EBITDA and distributable cash flow compared to Q3, driven by improved production and optimization activities.
  • Guidance for 2025 represents a notable increase from 2024 projections, supported by anticipated contributions from Corpus Christi Stage 3.
  • Management's tone during the Q4 call demonstrated increased confidence in achieving growth targets, contrasting with a more cautious outlook in Q3.
  • Analysts shifted focus from short-term production metrics in Q3 to broader strategic themes, including regulatory developments and long-term contracting opportunities.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential risks from geopolitical tensions impacting LNG markets, including reduced Russian pipeline flows to Europe and broader supply disruptions.
  • Delays in commissioning Corpus Christi Stage 3 could impact financial contributions in 2025. However, management expressed confidence in Bechtel's execution and the accelerated schedule.
  • Analysts raised questions about long-term market dynamics and competition for new contracts, which management addressed by emphasizing Cheniere's reliability and strong customer relationships.

Final Takeaway

Cheniere Energy's Q4 2024 results highlighted its robust financial performance and operational achievements, including record LNG production and significant progress on Corpus Christi Stage 3. Management's guidance for 2025 projects substantial EBITDA growth, supported by new production capacity and favorable market conditions. The company's disciplined capital allocation strategy and focus on long-term contracting position it well to capitalize on growing global LNG demand while navigating market uncertainties and geopolitical challenges.
